Pineapple Salsa

Jun 18, 2018

Ah!! Finally the man of the house gets a room in the blog. Oh! You got me wrong! He didn’t cook it or make it. It’s just that his favorites are getting some place in the blog. After he moved to the US, he developed certain attachment for Mexican cuisine. But it took me around 4 years to get the hang of it. I could cook it, but it’s only after I watched Pati’s Mexican Table hosted by Pati Jinich, I started liking the dishes more. The summer time in US is overloaded with tropical fruits, which makes you even crave for this kind of Mexican Salsa. One fine weekend, when I was clearing my kitchen shelves, I found a packet of tortilla chips sitting unnoticed. And then I went on to prepare this Pineapple Salsa for the side to the chips.

Believe me, fruit salsa like Mango and Pineapple go perfect for any summer-time snacking. All you need is a bowl full of salsa and few tortilla chips to munch on to spend the amazing sunny evening time in home. Without wasting a moment, I want to jot down this yummy delicious salsa.

Tortilla chips with a generous bite of salsa

Ingredients for Pineapple Salsa:

  1. Pineapple - 1 cup, finely chopped
  2. Red Bell Pepper - 1/4 cup, baked and chopped
  3. Hot Jalapenos - 1 , deseeded and finely chopped
  4. Coriander Leaves - 1/4 cup, finely chopped.
  5. Red Pearl Onions or Indian Shallots - 2 tbsp, finely chopped
  6. Garlic - 1 clove, finely grated
  7. Roasted Cumin Powder - 2 tsp
  8. Paprika Powder - 1 tsp
  9. Salt - according to taste
  10. Lime Juice - 2 tbsp

Steps of Pre - Preparation:

  1. Finely chop, the veggies as listed in the ingredient list. Make them as small as possible to give that chunky salsa texture.
  2. Bake the Red Bell Pepper at 400 F for 40 mins. And then chop it into equal size as other veggies.

Steps of Preparation:

  1. Bring a large mixing bowl, add all the ingredients.
  2. Mix all the ingredients so the spices and juices mix uniformly with all the ingredient. Serve it fresh to bring out the best of all the ingredients.

Pineapple Salsa

Pineapple Salsa

Recipe Type: Sides

Cuisine: Mexican

Author: Shreemaa Bhadra

Prep time: 10 mins

Total time: 10 mins

Serves: 4

Pineapple salsa - mix of chunky fresh Pineapples with the choice of veggies & herbs & some hot peppers, seasoned with Mexican spices like cumin & paprika.

Ingredients

  • Pineapple – 1 cup, finely chopped
  • Red Bell Pepper – 1/4 cup, baked and chopped
  • Hot Jalapenos – 1 , deseeded and finely chopped
  • Coriander Leaves – 1/4 cup, finely chopped.
  • Red Pearl Onions or Indian Shallots – 2 tbsp, finely chopped
  • Garlic – 1 clove, finely grated
  • Roasted Cumin Powder – 2 tsp
  • Paprika Powder – 1 tsp
  • Salt – according to taste
  • Lime Juice – 2 tbsp

Instructions

  1. Finely chop, the veggies as listed in the ingredient list. Make them as small as possible to give that chunky salsa texture.
  2. Bake the Red Bell Pepper at 400 F for 40 mins. And then chop it into equal size as other veggies.
  3. Bring a large mixing bowl, add all the ingredients.
  4. Mix all the ingredients so the spices and juices mix uniformly with all the ingredient. Serve it fresh to bring out the best of all the ingredients.
